content.rendered:
On this page, you can find an overview of articles that contain vocabulary that’s especially useful for beginners. Note that I tend to collect a lot of random vocabulary. Not all of it is especially important for you personally to know. I am simply listing words; it is your responsibility to choose which words you will need early on in your studies. There is also no reason to learn these topics in the order presented. Especially early on, it is good to cherry pick the vocabulary that is relevant to you personal rather than learning everything available by heart.

Vocabulary for beginners

FINNISH VOCABULARY FOR BEGINNERS

1. The very basics

    • Finnish loanwords
    • Survival Finnish
    • Classroom vocabulary
    • Your first 100 Finnish verbs
    • Country names
    • Famous places
    • Nationalities
    • Languages
    • Numbers

2. Adjectives

    • Colors
    • Adjectives and opposites

3. Expressions of time

    • Telling the time
    • The days of the week
    • The months

 4. Foods

    • Breakfast
    • Vegetables
    • Fruit
    • Berries
    • Herbs & spices
    • Meat
    • Beverages
    • Liquid foods
    • Dairy products

5. Body and health

    • Body parts
    • Bones and intestines
    • Illnesses
    • At the doctor’s

6. Animals

    • Birds
    • Fish and sea creatures
    • Insects
    • Mammals
    • Pets
    • Reptiles

7. How do I say?

    • “Thank you”
    • “Yes” and “no”
    • “I don’t know”
    • “How are you?”
    • “I have”
    • “I must”
    • “I like”

8. What’s the difference?

    • Soittaa leikkiä pelata: “To play” in Finnish
    • Osata Voida Saada: “I can” in Finnish
    • Tai and vai: “Or” in Finnish
    • Mikä and mitä: “What?” in Finnish

9. Special Finnish verbs

    • The verb “harrastaa“
    • The verb “käydä”

10. More beginner topics

    • Clothes
    • School
    • Talking about the weather
    • Places in the city
    • Rooms and furniture
    • Family members
